title: Tabernaemontana eusepaloides
intro: Tabernaemontana eusepaloides, also known as the Lesser Yellow Trumpet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant is reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for the treatment of various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana eusepaloides include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may be used to make tonics, syrups, and other preparations for the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana eusepaloides is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Thailand, Vietnam, and Laos. The classification of this plant is further divided into two subfamilies, Rauvolfioideae and Apocynoideae. The plant is also known by its other common name, Tabernaemontana, which is a genus of flowering plants within the Apocynaceae family.
